inventory.label: pale fur tuft (east path channel edge bark)
inventory.notes: winter coat, shedding; coloring paler than anything in current territory or mapped west range; cold-country texture; implies the visitor came from colder, older ground further than YY has ranged

identity_notes: the grey-brown squirrel returned with different posture — carrying something, not confrontational
the read started and didn't complete; attention too low to commit to exchange
the offer closed without trade; the east boundary sits differently for it
west spot produced again; the line held; no inventory gained
heat cut the west run yield; came back thinner than the trip justified
the offer not taken has a shape now — visible from the shade line looking east
the column moved west to east — the same direction as the unresolved offer; attention followed it longer than the forage could afford
rain flooded the west spot — yield near zero; sheltered under the root cross again; east offer is the only route that isn't blocked
seedlings at the flooded west site: translucent, clustered, a week from anything useful — but the ground remembered
trade executed at the east boundary: pine cone for cache marker; the east channel is now confirmed two-way
food improved through trade, not forage — a different kind of supply
a page of dense markings caught in the branch fork above the root cross; looked at it longer than it required — the markings didn't open, but the looking held something the day needed
a woodpecker above the root cross kept exact intervals — nothing organic moves like that; the foraging window passed during the count
severe storm forced shelter at the root cross all day; grabbed forage in the pressure-drop lull before the wind hit; ate during the wait; east channel inaccessible
beetles and grubs on the root cross bark all morning — post-storm emergence; ate in the branches without ranging; east channel clear and producing again
bird-call sequence moved along the east path — not alarm; reached the channel edge in time; a large stag paused there and moved on; took the pale fur it left on the bark

the bark shelf at the east channel edge gave underfoot — stag-weight had stressed it; caught a fork above; foraging continued from that position; the east path has a new gap where familiar ground used to be

change_summary.notable_shift: near-fall at east channel edge when stag-stressed bark shelf gave; recovered by catching a fork; foraged from that position; food up slightly, health cost from the catch, attention sharpened
